dataRoles.name: elementIds
dataRoles.description: Map the ElementId (Whole Number) column from the dataset

dataRoles.name: uniqueIds
dataRoles.description: Optional but recommended. Map the UniqueId (Text) column. When provided it is used as the primary stable key for resolving elements in Revit — survives purge and family reload, unlike ElementId.

dataRoles.name: documentTitle
dataRoles.description: Optional. Map the DocumentTitle (Text) column. When provided, RevitCortex verifies the active Revit document matches before any action — prevents accidentally selecting in the wrong open model.

dataRoles.name: colorBy
dataRoles.description: Optional. Drop any categorical field (e.g. Discipline, Family, Status, Workset). Each distinct value gets a deterministic color from the palette, applied as an override in Revit. Hex strings like '#E53935' are honored as-is.

dataRoles.name: highlightSync
dataRoles.description: Drop the same ElementId field used in 'Element ID' above. PBI auto-aggregates as Count — that's the only meaningful aggregation here. This binding is required by Power BI to propagate cross-filter highlights to the visual.
